title: Life Decisions as Code
problem: Software decisions get captured in feature.json files — problem, rationale, alternatives considered. But the same reasoning gap exists in life: people make major personal decisions (career changes, relationships, where to live) with no record of why. The context evaporates. Years later, you can't reconstruct the logic. Life-as-Code applies the same provenance model to personal decisions.

lineage.spawnReason: The same provenance model applied to software decisions applies equally to major life decisions — career changes, relocation, relationships. Life-as-Code extends the practice beyond code.

analysis: The feature.json model maps cleanly onto life decisions: every major choice has a problem it solves, alternatives that were seriously considered, and a rationale for the path taken. The difference is that life decisions feel too personal or too messy to document — but that's exactly when the context is most worth capturing. A career pivot, a relocation, a relationship choice: all have the same structure as a software architectural decision. This feature exists to make that parallel explicit and to show that LAC is not a developer tool — it's a reasoning tool.

decisions.decision: Spawn from the root feature rather than the ecosystem branch
decisions.rationale: This is a conceptual extension of the core idea, not a package or UI feature. It belongs at the same level as the app shell and the about section — part of the why, not the how.
alternativesConsidered: Spawn from feat-2026-004 (What is Life-as-Code) — too nested, loses visibility

decisions.decision: Use real-sounding personal examples as children rather than hypotheticals
decisions.rationale: Abstract examples don't land. A concrete career pivot or relocation decision makes the provenance model feel lived-in rather than academic.
alternativesConsidered: Generic placeholder examples — too sterile to make the point

implementation: No code — this is a content feature. feat-2026-017 and feat-2026-018 are the children that demonstrate the principle with concrete examples.
knownLimitations: The feature has no code artifact — the concept relies entirely on its children (feat-2026-017, feat-2026-018) to demonstrate it; without those rendered in the tree the feature reads as abstract
Whether the parallel between software and life decisions 'lands' for a reader is unobservable in the app — there is no measurable signal that the concept was understood

The model captures logic and rationale well but struggles with emotional and relational factors — a career pivot driven partly by burnout or a move driven partly by a relationship doesn't map cleanly to the decisions schema
successCriteria: A reader who is not a software developer looks at the career pivot and relocation child features and recognises the same reasoning structure they have used in their own major decisions. The parallel between a software architectural decision and a life decision is legible from the card alone — no explanation required.
